title: The Ugly Duckling
author: Hans Christian Andersen
description: A little bird looks different from everyone else. He feels sad and alone. But then something wonderful happens.

vocabularies.meaning: one of the light things that cover a bird's body
a large white bird with a long neck
to come out of an egg

scenes.title: The Egg
scenes.narrator: Once upon a time, a mother duck sat on her eggs. She waited and waited. Finally, the eggs started to hatch.

scenes.title: The Hatching
scenes.narrator: One by one, baby ducks came out of the eggs. They were small and yellow. But one egg did not hatch.

dialogue.speaker: Mother Duck
dialogue.line: Come out, little one! What is taking so long?

scenes.title: The Ugly Duckling
scenes.narrator: Finally, the last egg hatched. But this duckling was different. He was big and gray. He looked strange.

dialogue.speaker: Other Ducklings
dialogue.line: Look at him! He is so ugly!

dialogue.speaker: Mother Duck
dialogue.line: He is different, but he is still my baby.

scenes.title: Rejection
scenes.narrator: The other animals did not like the ugly duckling. They laughed at him.

dialogue.speaker: Chicken
dialogue.line: You are too big! Go away!

dialogue.speaker: Pig
dialogue.line: You are too gray! You are ugly!

scenes.title: Sadness
scenes.narrator: The ugly duckling felt very sad. He decided to leave home.

dialogue.speaker: Ugly Duckling
dialogue.line: Nobody likes me. I am going away.

scenes.title: In the Wild
scenes.narrator: The ugly duckling walked and walked. He was alone in the world.

dialogue.speaker: Ugly Duckling
dialogue.line: I am so lonely. I have no friends.

scenes.title: Winter
scenes.narrator: Winter came. It was very cold. The ugly duckling found a small house.

dialogue.speaker: Ugly Duckling
dialogue.line: It is so cold! Can I come in?

dialogue.speaker: Farmer
dialogue.line: Come in, little bird. But be careful with my children.

scenes.title: Spring
scenes.narrator: Winter ended. Spring came. The ugly duckling left the house and went to the pond.

scenes.title: The Swans
scenes.narrator: At the pond, the ugly duckling saw beautiful birds. They were swans.

dialogue.speaker: Ugly Duckling
dialogue.line: They are so beautiful. I wish I could be like them.

scenes.title: The Reflection
scenes.narrator: The ugly duckling looked into the water. He saw his reflection. But something was different.

dialogue.speaker: Ugly Duckling
dialogue.line: Wait! That is not me! I am... I am a swan!

scenes.title: Transformation
scenes.narrator: The ugly duckling was not a duck at all. He was a swan! He was beautiful!

dialogue.speaker: Other Swans
dialogue.line: Welcome! You are one of us!

scenes.title: Happy Ending
scenes.narrator: The ugly duckling was not ugly at all. He was a beautiful swan. He was finally happy.

dialogue.speaker: Ugly Duckling
dialogue.line: I am proud of who I am.
